name: geopandas
tagline: Teaches GeoPandas usage for reading, transforming, analyzing, and mapping geospatial vector data (shapefiles, GeoJSON, PostGIS).
verdict: Clean, low-risk library-usage skill. No credential access, no network exfiltration, no shell/curl-pipe execution, no obfuscation or hidden instructions. Package installs (geopandas, folium, pyarrow, psycopg2, cartopy, etc.) are all mainstream PyPI packages via `uv pip install`, matching the stated geospatial-analysis scope. Minor quality gaps: no explicit ne

highlights.summary: No 'do not use when' guidance is given (e.g. raster data, non-geospatial tabular pandas work); minor omission typical for library-reference skills.
No explicit statement of expected output/deliverable format beyond the code examples themselves (e.g. what a completed task should look like).

Failure-path handling is thin: only a one-line best practice to 'Validate geometries with `.is_valid`' (line 243); no guidance on handling CRS mismatches, malformed files, or overwrite-on-write errors
